title: Trust the Batch, On- or Off-Policy: Adaptive Policy Optimization for RL Post-Training
audience: ML/RL students who know policy gradients and log probabilities.
central_question: How can a policy optimizer adapt its update when replay data becomes stale?

teaching_units.name: Policy Ratios Reveal Staleness
teaching_units.goal: Connect stored old logprobs to ratio spread under old-vs-new policy mismatch.
teaching_units.visual_example: Fresh ratios cluster near 1; stale replay creates a long-tailed ratio distribution.

teaching_units.name: Normalized Effective Sample Size
teaching_units.goal: Show how one dominant ratio shrinks the effective batch.
teaching_units.visual_example: A five-row ratio table morphs from balanced to dominated; the ESS gauge drops.

teaching_units.name: P3O Adaptive Objective
teaching_units.goal: Explain score-function capping and behavioral KL as two uses of ESS.
formulas: loss = - stopgrad(rho) * log pi * A + (1 - ESS) * KL(pi || pi_old)
teaching_units.visual_example: As ESS falls, the score cap lowers and behavioral KL weight increases.

comparison_axes: PPO/GRPO fixed clipping vs P3O ESS-adaptive capping
fresh sync training vs stale async replay
concept formula vs implementation code path
